categories.title: Miami Beach Tours
categories.description: Beach-first experiences that help visitors choose between South Beach energy, Key Biscayne reset time, and water-led Miami days.
categories.intro: Beach planning in Miami works best when the page answers the real comparison first: iconic South Beach energy, a cleaner Key Biscayne escape, a boat-heavy bay day, or a lower-friction coastal route that still feels worth leaving the hotel for. This page should lead with those lanes instead of broad sightseeing noise.
categories.inventoryTitle: Top Miami Beach Tours Right Now
bullets: Best fit for travelers choosing one strong beach or water block instead of a generic Miami sightseeing list
Useful for comparing South Beach energy, Key Biscayne downtime, bay cruises, and easier half-day coastal options
Easy to pair with a food stop, sunset cruise, or one cleaner waterfront day in a short trip
Strong option when the decision is about beach feel, water access, and pace rather than a broad city tour

intents.label: South Beach highlights
intents.description: The clearest lane for travelers who want the iconic beach scene, art deco context, and the highest-energy Miami coastline.

intents.label: Key Biscayne beach escape
intents.query: key biscayne beach tour from miami
intents.description: Best for visitors who want a calmer sand-and-water reset away from South Beach crowds.

intents.label: Biscayne Bay beach and boat day
intents.query: miami biscayne bay boat and beach tour
intents.description: Useful when the trip should mix skyline water views with a more activity-led bay block.

intents.label: Sandbar and coastal half-day
intents.query: miami sandbar boat beach half day
intents.description: A strong fallback for shorter planning windows when the goal is easy beach access and a lighter water itinerary.
